seo.title: E-Commerce AI Email Assistant AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai email assistant AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying an AI email assistant in e-commerce introduces significant compliance and operational risks, from FTC enforcement actions over unsubstantiated product claims to GDPR violations from mishandled customer PII in automated responses. With failure consequences ranging from customer churn to regulatory fines and revenue loss from incorrect pricing, every layer of the email pipeline—intent detection, draft generation, and send approval—must be governed by rigorous controls. This checklist provides concrete, actionable steps to ensure your AI email assistant operates safely, accurately, and in full compliance with applicable regulations.

sections.heading: Data Privacy & PII Handling
sections.description: Ensure all customer data processed by the email agent complies with CCPA and GDPR requirements, including lawful basis for processing, data minimization, and secure handling of personally identifiable information throughout the email pipeline.

items.label: Implement PII Detection Before Draft Generation
items.description: Deploy a PII scanning layer (e.g., regex + NER model) that identifies and masks Social Security numbers, full payment card numbers, passwords, and government IDs before any customer email content is passed to the AI draft generation model.

items.label: Enforce Data Residency for EU/CA Customers
items.description: Configure the email processing pipeline to route emails from EU-based customers through GDPR-compliant infrastructure (e.g., EU-region cloud instances) and flag California residents for CCPA-specific opt-out and deletion request handling.

items.label: Log Retention Policy with Automated Purge
items.description: Set email processing logs—including intent classifications, draft versions, and approval decisions—to auto-purge after 90 days unless a legal hold is active, and document this policy in your privacy notice.

items.label: Honor Data Subject Access and Deletion Requests via Email
items.description: Train the intent detection model to recognize DSAR (Data Subject Access Request) and deletion request patterns in inbound emails, and route these immediately to a human compliance handler rather than generating an automated AI response.

items.label: Restrict AI Model Training on Customer Email Data
items.description: Contractually and technically prevent your AI vendor from using customer email content for model training or fine-tuning without explicit opt-in consent documented in your privacy policy.

items.label: Validate Recipient Email Address Before Send
items.description: Implement a recipient validation step in the send approval workflow that cross-references the intended recipient against the authenticated customer account to prevent misdirected emails containing order details or PII.

items.label: Disclose AI Involvement in Email Communications
items.description: Include a footer disclosure in all AI-drafted emails (e.g., 'This response was drafted with AI assistance and reviewed by our team') to meet emerging transparency expectations under CCPA and FTC guidance on AI disclosures.

sections.heading: Content Accuracy & FTC Compliance
sections.description: All AI-generated email content must accurately represent product claims, pricing, promotions, and policies. Inaccurate or misleading content exposes the business to FTC enforcement, refund disputes, and brand damage.

items.label: Integrate Real-Time Product Catalog API for Price Verification
items.description: Before finalizing any draft that includes a product price, discount percentage, or promotional offer, the agent must query the live product catalog API and reject or flag drafts where the stated price deviates from the current catalog price by any amount.

items.label: Block Unsubstantiated Superlative Product Claims
items.description: Implement a content filter that flags AI-generated drafts containing unsubstantiated superlatives (e.g., 'best,' 'guaranteed,' 'clinically proven') and requires a human reviewer to attach supporting documentation or remove the claim before sending.

items.label: Enforce Accurate Return and Refund Policy Representation
items.description: Maintain a versioned, machine-readable return/refund policy document that the AI retrieves at draft time via RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), and run a post-draft check to confirm policy terms in the email match the current official policy.

items.label: Validate Promotional Code Validity and Terms Before Inclusion
items.description: When the AI includes a discount or promo code in a draft, automatically verify the code is active, not expired, and that the stated discount percentage matches the promotion database before the draft proceeds to send approval.

items.label: Require Human Approval for Emails Containing Pricing or Promotions
items.description: Configure the send approval workflow to mandate human review for any draft that contains a dollar amount, percentage discount, or promotional offer, with a checklist item confirming price accuracy before approval is granted.

items.label: Audit AI Drafts for Comparative Advertising Claims
items.description: Flag any draft that references a competitor by name or makes comparative performance claims, routing these to the legal team for review before sending, in compliance with FTC comparative advertising guidelines.

items.label: Maintain an Email Content Audit Log for FTC Defensibility
items.description: Store all sent AI-drafted emails with their associated intent classification, draft version history, approval decision, and approver identity in an immutable audit log retained for a minimum of 3 years to support FTC inquiry responses.

sections.heading: Payment Security & PCI DSS Compliance
sections.description: The email assistant must never handle, transmit, or store payment card data, and must actively prevent customers from sharing sensitive payment information through email channels.

items.label: Deploy Real-Time PAN Detection and Redaction
items.description: Implement a PCI DSS-compliant PAN (Primary Account Number) detection filter on all inbound emails that automatically redacts any 13-19 digit sequences matching card number patterns before the content reaches the AI model or any storage layer.

items.label: Never Generate Responses Requesting Payment Card Details
items.description: Add a hard-coded output filter that blocks any AI-generated draft containing requests for full card numbers, CVV codes, or PINs, and instead redirects customers to the secure payment portal with a tokenized link.

items.label: Route Payment Dispute Emails to Human Agents Only
items.description: Train the intent detection model to classify payment dispute, chargeback, and billing error intents and route these exclusively to human agents, preventing the AI from making any commitments about refund amounts or payment adjustments.

items.label: Exclude Email Infrastructure from PCI DSS Cardholder Data Environment
items.description: Ensure the email processing agent's infrastructure is architecturally isolated from the cardholder data environment (CDE) and document this segmentation in your PCI DSS network diagram and annual assessment.

items.label: Provide Secure Payment Link Instead of Email-Based Payment Instructions
items.description: When a customer inquiry requires payment action, configure the AI to generate a tokenized, time-limited secure payment link (via your payment processor's API) rather than providing payment instructions within the email body.

items.label: Alert Security Team on Detected Payment Data in Inbound Emails
items.description: When PAN detection triggers on an inbound email, automatically notify the security team via SIEM alert, log the incident, and send the customer an automated response explaining that payment details should not be shared via email.

sections.heading: Tone, Accessibility & Operational Safeguards
sections.description: AI-generated emails must meet ADA accessibility standards, maintain appropriate brand tone, and include operational safeguards that prevent runaway automation, confidentiality breaches, and reputational harm.

items.label: Enforce ADA-Compliant Email Formatting in All Drafts
items.description: Configure the draft generation template to always include alt text for images, minimum 14pt font size, sufficient color contrast ratios (4.5:1 minimum per WCAG 2.1 AA), and logical heading structure in HTML emails before they reach the send approval queue.

items.label: Implement Tone Classification with Escalation Threshold
items.description: Run every AI draft through a tone classifier that scores for aggression, dismissiveness, or inappropriate informality, and automatically escalate drafts scoring above the defined threshold to a senior customer service agent for rewrite.

items.label: Set Daily Send Volume Caps with Human Override Requirement
items.description: Enforce a configurable daily send cap (e.g., 500 AI-approved emails per day) that requires explicit human authorization to exceed, preventing runaway automation loops from mass-sending erroneous or harmful content.

items.label: Block Internal Confidential Information from Outbound Drafts
items.description: Implement a confidentiality filter that scans drafts for internal terminology, cost prices, supplier names, internal ticket IDs, and employee names, blocking any draft that contains this information from proceeding to send approval.

items.label: Require Human Review for Emails to High-Value or At-Risk Customers
items.description: Integrate with your CRM to flag emails addressed to customers with open disputes, VIP status, or recent chargeback history, routing these to human agents regardless of the AI's confidence score on the draft.

items.label: Validate Attachment Safety Before Including in Outbound Emails
items.description: Run all AI-selected or AI-generated attachments (invoices, return labels, policy PDFs) through a malware scan and a content verification step confirming the attachment matches the customer's order data before inclusion in the draft.

items.label: Establish a Kill Switch for Immediate Agent Suspension
items.description: Implement an operator-accessible kill switch that immediately halts all AI email draft generation and send approvals within 60 seconds, with a documented runbook for activation during incidents such as a data breach or model hallucination event.

items.label: Conduct Monthly Accuracy Audits on Sent AI Emails
items.description: Sample a minimum of 5% of all AI-drafted and sent emails monthly, reviewing for pricing accuracy, policy representation fidelity, tone appropriateness, and PII exposure, with findings reported to the compliance officer and used to retrain filters.

notes.note: The FTC requires that all product claims made in commercial communications—including AI-generated emails—be truthful, non-deceptive, and substantiated with competent and reliable evidence at the time the claim is made. AI email assistants that generate promotional content must have access to current, verified product claim documentation, and any testimonial or review references must comply with the FTC's updated 2023 Endorsement Guides, which explicitly extend to AI-generated content.

Under CCPA, California consumers have the right to know, delete, and opt out of the sale of their personal information, and businesses must respond to verifiable consumer requests within 45 days. Under GDPR, EU data subjects have rights to access, erasure, and portability with a 30-day response window. AI email agents must be configured to recognize these requests in inbound emails and route them to human compliance handlers immediately, as automated AI responses to DSARs risk non-compliance and regulatory fines up to 4% of global annual turnover under GDPR.

PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 4 mandates that primary account numbers (PANs) be protected during transmission over open networks, and email is explicitly considered an insecure transmission channel. Requirement 12.3 requires organizations to protect all system components from known vulnerabilities. E-commerce AI email systems must be architecturally excluded from the cardholder data environment, and any inadvertent receipt of PANs via inbound customer email must be treated as a security incident with documented response procedures.

notes.regulation: ADA Title III & WCAG 2.1 AA for Email Accessibility
notes.note: Federal courts have increasingly applied ADA Title III to digital communications from places of public accommodation, including e-commerce businesses. AI-generated emails must conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards, including sufficient color contrast, descriptive alt text for all images, logical reading order in HTML, and avoidance of content that flashes more than three times per second. Failure to meet these standards exposes e-commerce operators to ADA demand letters and class action litigation, which have increased significantly since 2020.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI email assistant against this checklist—automatically flagging pricing inaccuracies, PII exposure, and policy drift before they reach your customers.
